columns.title: Index and query all four Yazelix repositories
columns.goal: Build fresh GitNexus indexes with PDG and derive repository ownership seams before planning runtime changes.
columns.inputs: /home/flexnetos/meta/.meta.yaml; four Yazelix peer working trees
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/EVIDENCE.md; four .gitnexus indexes
columns.blocked_paths: tracked product-source mutation; profile mutation; generated Yazelix runtime mutation
columns.verification_gate: GitNexus status is up-to-date for all four repos and repo-qualified query/context calls return the recorded ownership seams.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the four disposable .gitnexus indexes and this baseline proof; tracked repository source remains unchanged.
columns.next_action: Ratify the target ownership contract in YZXCONV-002.

columns.title: Ratify one source-to-runtime ownership contract
columns.goal: Define exact editable-input, generated-output, runtime-state, profile, fleet, and peer boundaries before implementation.
columns.inputs: yazelix_runtime_convergence/EVIDENCE.md; Yazelix ownership docs; Meta AGENTS.md; envctl AGENTS.md
columns.target_artifacts: approved ownership decision; path and command authority matrix
columns.blocked_paths: profile mutation; global config mutation; peer implementation code
columns.verification_gate: Owner approves the matrix in PLAN.md and every active path has exactly one editable owner, one generator, and one runtime consumer.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert the decision document and keep all current runtime selectors unchanged.
columns.next_action: Owner ratified all 14 PLAN.md ownership clauses on 2026-07-14; proceed to YZXCONV-003 single-profile source contract under per-step approval.

columns.title: Converge to one active Nix/Yazelix profile closure
columns.goal: Make ~/.nix-profile the sole interactive foundation selector and prevent the XDG profile from selecting a different closure or adding a second owner.
columns.inputs: approved ownership decision; current two-profile receipt; staged Home Manager-in-foundation work
columns.target_artifacts: single-profile package contract; migration and rollback receipt
columns.blocked_paths: /nix/store mutation outside Nix; deleting profile generations without approval; hand-editing /home/flexnetos/.local/share/yazelix/**
columns.verification_gate: nix profile list shows one foundation element; ~/.nix-profile is the only PATH owner; any XDG selector resolves to the same closure or is inactive; yzx doctor reports one install owner.
columns.rollback_plan: Atomically restore the pre-change profile symlink recorded in the migration receipt and keep the prior generation reachable.
columns.next_action: Finish source package contracts, build without profile mutation, then request approval for cutover.

columns.title: Converge Codex config authorship while preserving its real runtime home
columns.goal: Keep the profile-owned Codex binary and ~/.codex runtime/state home while materializing config.toml from a reviewed Yazelix-owned editable input and eliminating old-profile references.
columns.inputs: Codex doctor receipt; approved input path; Yazelix runtime model; yazelix agent.command and agent.args popup launch keys
columns.target_artifacts: Codex config input; materializer; runtime provenance gate
columns.blocked_paths: /home/flexnetos/.codex/auth.json; /home/flexnetos/.codex/sessions/**; /home/flexnetos/.codex/*.sqlite*; retired workspace .codex mirrors
columns.verification_gate: codex doctor reports the profile executable, CODEX_HOME=/home/flexnetos/.codex, parseable generated config, and no path into an inactive profile or retired workspace mirror.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior config.toml from a checksum-backed copy without moving auth, sessions, or databases.
columns.next_action: Implement the approved config materializer after YZXCONV-003 source proof.

columns.title: Converge Claude settings authorship through the Yazelix/envctl chain
columns.goal: Keep the profile-owned Claude binary and ~/.claude state home while replacing direct settings authorship with reviewed Yazelix input and envctl-compatible materialization.
columns.inputs: Claude binary receipt; approved input path; envctl agent-env contract; yazelix agent.command and agent.args popup launch keys
columns.target_artifacts: Claude settings input; materializer; runtime provenance gate
columns.blocked_paths: /home/flexnetos/.claude credentials; /home/flexnetos/.claude history and cache; retired workspace .claude mirrors
columns.verification_gate: Claude launches from the one profile, settings checksum back to the approved input, generated hooks contain no stale Bash-to-Nu wrapper, and state remains under ~/.claude.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the previous settings.json from a checksum-backed copy without moving credentials or history.
columns.next_action: Implement beside YZXCONV-004 so both runtimes consume one ownership contract.

columns.title: Package the native envctl agent-env binary in the single profile
columns.goal: Expose the native envctl binary through Yazelix toolbin/bin and remove the generated /bin/sh Meta wrapper from the active interactive route.
columns.inputs: meta/usr/bin/envctl wrapper receipt; native meta/usr/libexec binary; envctl manifests
columns.target_artifacts: Yazelix package input; native envctl profile command; Meta projection update
columns.blocked_paths: global package-manager install; user-local envctl wrapper; hand-edited generated runtime
columns.verification_gate: command -v envctl resolves under ~/.nix-profile; readlink reaches a native executable; no active envctl route depends on bash/sh/zsh; envctl --version and lock preview pass.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior Meta wrapper/projection and remove only the new unselected profile generation.
columns.next_action: Build envctl from its clean worktree and consume the proven artifact in Yazelix.

columns.title: Add preview-first Meta fleet agent-env policy and sync
columns.goal: Give the Meta root and every declared src peer an explicit agent-env config or exclusion and drive preview/apply only through Meta and envctl frontdoors.
columns.inputs: tasks/meta-envctl-fleet-agent-env-policy; .meta.yaml; envctl peer sync receipt
columns.target_artifacts: Meta fleet policy; per-peer result report; reviewed apply receipt
columns.blocked_paths: implicit peer config generation; project .codex/.claude hand edits; apply without approval; generated Yazelix runtime edits
columns.verification_gate: Meta-root lock check passes; preview reports every selected peer; reviewed apply has zero broken/failed assets; a second preview is idempotent; exclusions have rationales.
columns.rollback_plan: Use envctl's operation receipt to restore affected projections and the prior lock; never roll back unrelated peer commits together.
columns.next_action: Implement the active Meta GitKB task as a reviewed fleet transaction.

columns.title: Codify Meta central control and independent peer mode
columns.goal: Make meta git and meta exec the fleet frontdoors while preserving local Git, issue, test, release, and commit authority in every peer.
columns.inputs: Meta GitKB architecture; .meta.yaml; four repo status receipts
columns.target_artifacts: dual-mode contract; command matrix; regression checks
columns.blocked_paths: monorepo conversion; cross-repo squashed history; raw fleet mutation outside Meta frontdoors
columns.verification_gate: meta git status covers the fleet; meta exec can target the four Yazelix peers; each peer local git status and repo-specific workflow remains functional and independently committable.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the policy/test commit in its owning repository; peer histories remain untouched.
columns.next_action: Land the dual-mode contract before fleet sync implementation.

columns.title: Implement and select RTK TokenKill nu_shell mode
columns.goal: Add a documented native nu_shell configuration mode to rtk-tokenkill and select it in the Yazelix-owned active RTK config.
columns.inputs: rtk 0.43.0 config receipt; rtk-tokenkill source; Yazelix Nu contract
columns.target_artifacts: rtk nu_shell schema and tests; Yazelix config input; installed proof
columns.blocked_paths: legacy shell rewrite hook restoration; global cargo install; direct generated-runtime edit
columns.verification_gate: rtk config prints the documented nu_shell selection; native Nu integration tests pass; Codex and Claude commands route through the profile RTK binary without Bash rewrite hooks.
columns.rollback_plan: Re-select the prior documented RTK mode and restore its config input; keep the previous profile generation available.
columns.next_action: Design the rtk config schema in its independent repo, publish it, then pin it in Yazelix.

columns.title: Complete the Yazelix native Nushell command catalog
columns.goal: Make yazelix/nushell/config the single interactive command catalog for codex, claude, envctl, meta, grit, icm, weave, nix, bun, bunx, and raw gate escapes.
columns.inputs: staged rtk_wrappers.nu; profile command inventory; RTK policy
columns.target_artifacts: native Nu modules and externs; package contracts; behavior tests
columns.blocked_paths: npm/npx/pnpm as active package owners; generated ~/.local/share/yazelix edits; duplicate host command definitions
columns.verification_gate: Nu parses every module; command signatures/argv survive; meta/grit/icm/weave/envctl/nix resolve through profile paths; bun/bunx replace npm/npx/pnpm; caret raw gates remain available.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the new Nu imports/modules and restore the prior packaged config; do not restore user-bin wrappers.
columns.next_action: Revise the staged wrapper module rather than layering a second command catalog.

columns.title: Retire or explicitly bridge every legacy Bash POSIX and Zsh route
columns.goal: Classify every active shell launcher/wrapper; delete stale ones and make each retained POSIX boundary callable and testable from the Yazelix Nu catalog without alternate ownership.
columns.inputs: Yazelix shells/{bash,posix,zsh}; Meta usr/bin wrappers; Claude hook inventory
columns.target_artifacts: shell boundary inventory; deletions; Nu bridge tests; retained-boundary rationale
columns.blocked_paths: unreviewed deletion of platform bootstrap; shell rewrites outside owner repos; generated runtime hand edits
columns.verification_gate: No stale bash-to-nu or user-local wrapper exists; no active Zsh/Bash command lacks a Nu equivalent; retained sh boundaries use fixed code plus structured argv and have exact behavior tests.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore only a removed boundary whose pre-change behavior receipt proves it is required; never reintroduce an alternate profile owner.
columns.next_action: Create the inventory from Meta canonical repos, excluding caches and runner workdirs.

columns.title: Add Nu surfaces for Meta Grit ICM Weave and canonicalize old flakes
columns.goal: Give Meta, Grit, ICM, Weave, and Nix/flake operations first-party Nu externs/modules and classify duplicate or obsolete flake roots through Meta inventory.
columns.inputs: profile command receipts; flake inventory; peer repositories
columns.target_artifacts: Nu modules; canonical flake registry; stale-flake retirement plan and proof
columns.blocked_paths: runner _work trees; temporary worktrees; vendored upstream source mutation; deleting Nix generations
columns.verification_gate: Nu extern/help smoke tests pass for all five command families; Meta inventory labels every flake canonical/vendor/worktree/runner/stale; only approved stale control paths are retired.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore retired tracked flake files in their independent repos and remove only the new Nu imports if verification fails.
columns.next_action: Separate canonical peer flakes from worktree, runner, and vendored copies before editing.

columns.title: Verify and pin the four-repository ownership transaction
columns.goal: Ensure main Yazelix consumes reviewed child revisions while Helix, Yazi Assets, and Terminal Support remain independently buildable and narrowly owned.
columns.inputs: four GitNexus graphs; child AGENTS.md files; main flake inputs
columns.target_artifacts: published child revisions; main lock/pin update; independent build receipts
columns.blocked_paths: cross-repo uncommitted override as final proof; child runtime-policy expansion; monorepo commit
columns.verification_gate: Each child local gate passes; child commits are published before the main pin; main validation passes without override-input; GitNexus confirms the intended ownership seams.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the main input pin to the last published child revisions; preserve independently valid child commits.
columns.next_action: Implement child changes first only where a task proves they are necessary.

columns.parent_id: YZXCONV-003; YZXCONV-004; YZXCONV-005; YZXCONV-006; YZXCONV-007; YZXCONV-009; YZXCONV-010; YZXCONV-011; YZXCONV-012; YZXCONV-013
columns.title: Build install materialize and test the one-profile runtime
columns.goal: Prove the complete source-to-profile-to-generated-runtime chain for yzx, Nu, RTK, Codex, Claude, envctl, Meta, Grit, ICM, Weave, and terminals.
columns.inputs: all prior task proofs; built foundation closure; approved cutover
columns.target_artifacts: installed runtime receipt; generated config checksums; doctor and command evidence
columns.blocked_paths: auth and secret files; sessions and databases; manual generated-runtime edits; stale user-bin shadows
columns.verification_gate: Source package checks pass; one profile is selected; yzx doctor/inspect pass; generated Nu loads nu_shell and all command modules; Codex/Claude/envctl provenance is correct; Kitty/Ghostty contract passes.
columns.rollback_plan: Atomically reselect the prior profile generation and restore config projections from receipts; retain failure logs outside generated runtime.
columns.next_action: Run build proof before requesting the explicit profile/config cutover approval.

columns.title: Reindex verify and close the convergence graph
columns.goal: Audit every explicit requirement against current source, installed runtime, fleet state, and independent peer state before declaring completion.
columns.inputs: all YZXCONV proofs; installed runtime; four repositories; Meta fleet
columns.target_artifacts: final requirement matrix; refreshed GitNexus indexes; clean-state receipts
columns.allowed_paths: /home/flexnetos/meta/src/lifeos/planning-spine-v0/yazelix_runtime_convergence/**; four Yazelix .gitnexus indexes; read-only fleet and runtime surfaces
columns.blocked_paths: new implementation mutation; unapproved push; deletion of unrelated dirty work; completion with missing proof
columns.verification_gate: All 15 task gates have proof; four indexes are current; meta git status and gh open-PR inventory are reconciled; peer local statuses are clean or explicitly attributed; no requirement is missing or indirect.
columns.rollback_plan: Reopen the first contradicted task and invalidate dependent proof records; do not paper over the contradiction in the final matrix.
columns.next_action: Execute only after YZXCONV-014 installed-runtime proof passes.

columns.title: Make Kache the only agent and workspace cache authority
columns.goal: Route every agent and workspace cache through Kache, remove or disable competing cache authorities, and keep the Nix store and the one Yazelix profile as install ownership rather than parallel cache ownership.
columns.inputs: owner Kache-only directive; Yazelix kache package; fleet cache inventory; runner and package-manager cache settings
columns.target_artifacts: Kache authority matrix; migrated cache configuration; competing-cache retirement receipts
columns.blocked_paths: new npm pnpm Bun sccache ccache runner or ad hoc target cache authority; global package install; generated runtime hand edits
columns.verification_gate: A fleet inventory proves every enabled cache path and cache environment variable is owned or mediated by Kache; no agent command creates a competing package build compiler runner or ad hoc cache; all cache and tool binaries resolve through the one profile.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore only checksum-backed pre-migration cache settings for a failed component, disable that component's agent execution, and keep the contradiction open until Kache owns it.
columns.next_action: Inventory every cache producer and write the failing Kache-only contract tests before changing configuration.

columns.title: Enforce Nushell as the only agent shell
columns.goal: Require every agent shell action to enter through the profile-owned Nushell; reserve Bash sh Zsh Fish and Xonsh for the owner only, with a per-incident owner-authorized emergency fallback allowed solely after a system crash.
columns.inputs: owner Nushell-only directive; shell boundary inventory; RTK nu_shell work; agent launcher and hook configuration
columns.target_artifacts: strict agent-shell policy; launcher and hook enforcement; emergency approval receipt schema; negative tests
columns.blocked_paths: agent invocation of bash sh zsh fish xonsh; inferred fallback permission; alternate shell owner; generated runtime hand edits
columns.verification_gate: Agent launchers hooks skills tests and receipts prove profile Nushell is the sole shell route; negative tests reject every other shell; any emergency route requires an explicit owner approval tied to a documented system crash and is removed after recovery.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior profile generation if Nushell prevents recovery, disable agents, and permit a temporary alternate shell only when the owner explicitly approves that crash incident.
columns.next_action: Add failing enforcement tests to Yazelix and envctl before retiring alternate agent shell routes.

columns.title: Encode the owner contract and task graph in envctl agent-env
columns.goal: Add YZXCONV-016 through YZXCONV-022 and their Kache, Nushell, profile, cleanup, musl, and Home Manager gates to the canonical envctl agent-env source, lock, generated projections, harness guidance, and validation tests.
columns.inputs: owner contract; YZXCONV task graph; envctl AGENTS contracts; current agent-env source and lock
columns.target_artifacts: reviewed agent-env policy and lock; generated projection receipts; harness tests linked to task IDs
columns.blocked_paths: direct generated-runtime edits; apply without preview and review; duplicate Codex config authority; task omission
columns.verification_gate: envctl lock check preview and policy tests pass; the source and generated guidance name YZXCONV-016 through YZXCONV-022; a second preview is idempotent; installed proof resolves through the one profile without widening plugin or MCP authority.
columns.rollback_plan: Use the envctl operation receipt to restore the prior source lock and projections, then rerun preview; do not preserve a partial projection as a second authority.
columns.next_action: Run after the Kache and Nushell red tests define the exact envctl policy surface.

columns.title: Settle and purge every worktree stash branch and pull request
columns.goal: Commit every valuable stash and worktree change, push every branch through a pull request without cherry-picking, merge all approved work, synchronize origin and main master and develop where those branches exist, then purge merged branches worktrees and stashes.
columns.inputs: Meta fleet inventory; all local worktrees branches stashes remotes and open pull requests; repository-owned checks
columns.target_artifacts: per-repository commits and pull requests; merge receipts; synchronized branch proofs; clean worktree branch and stash inventory
columns.allowed_paths: /home/flexnetos/meta/**; declared peer repositories; repository-owned worktree cleanup source; GitHub branches and pull requests
columns.blocked_paths: cherry-pick; silent stash deletion; unpushed change; draft task substitute; force rewrite; deleting unmerged or unattributed work
columns.verification_gate: Every discovered change is committed and pushed; every branch has a merged pull request or an explicit owner disposition; no cherry-pick appears in the transaction; origin and each existing main master develop ref agree; git status worktree list branch list stash list and open-PR inventory are clean; merged local and remote branches and temporary worktrees are purged.
columns.rollback_plan: Stop before deleting any unattributed state; recreate only from the published branch or pull request receipt; never use a stash or cherry-pick as the final recovery mechanism.
columns.next_action: Preserve each dirty repository independently, publish reviewable pull requests, merge in dependency order, and run the cleanup gate after each merge.

columns.parent_id: YZXCONV-014; YZXCONV-015; YZXCONV-016; YZXCONV-017; YZXCONV-018; YZXCONV-019; YZXCONV-021; YZXCONV-022
columns.title: Prove the enforced owner contract and close the fleet
columns.goal: Independently prove Kache-only caching, Nushell-only agent execution, one Yazelix profile ownership, canonical envctl inclusion, Yazelix musl portability, Home Manager single-owner integration, and fully synchronized clean repositories before any completion claim.
columns.inputs: all YZXCONV proofs; installed runtime; envctl projections; musl and closure receipts; Home Manager ownership receipt; Meta fleet; GitHub merge and cleanup receipts
columns.target_artifacts: final owner-contract matrix; installed-runtime receipt; portability receipt; Home Manager ownership receipt; clean synchronized fleet receipt; invalidation map
columns.allowed_paths: /home/flexnetos/meta/src/lifeos/planning-spine-v0/yazelix_runtime_convergence/**; read-only installed runtime and fleet surfaces; four refreshed Yazelix indexes
columns.blocked_paths: new implementation mutation; missing proof; alternate shell, cache, profile, or Home Manager authority; false universal-static claim; dirty or divergent repo; open mergeable cleanup pull request
columns.verification_gate: All 22 task gates have current proof; Kache is the only cache authority; agents execute shell actions only through profile Nushell; one profile owns binaries, runtime, Home Manager, and active configs; eligible Yazelix binaries have musl proof and the remaining closure has an honest portable fallback; envctl names every closure task; all repositories and GitHub refs are synchronized clean and purged.
columns.rollback_plan: Invalidate this proof and reopen the earliest contradicted task; keep downstream proofs blocked until the installed runtime and fleet return to the exact owner contract.
columns.next_action: Execute only after YZXCONV-019, YZXCONV-021, and YZXCONV-022 have current proof.

columns.title: Prove Yazelix musl portability without false universal-static claims
columns.goal: Use TDD to establish x86_64-unknown-linux-musl builds for every eligible Yazelix and LifeOS Rust binary, classify GUI, Nix, native, and glibc-only dependencies that cannot be static-musl, and prove one honest portable closure or bundle fallback for the remaining runtime.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint Nix plus musl requirements; ARCHITECTURE_BLUEPRINT_COMPATIBILITY.md; YZXCONV-003; four-repository ownership receipt; current Yazelix flake and release contracts
columns.target_artifacts: musl support matrix; failing-then-passing packaging tests; reproducible binary receipts; non-musl dependency disposition; portable closure or bundle receipt
columns.blocked_paths: profile mutation before build proof; generated Yazelix runtime edits; global installs; unsupported universal-static claims; replacing the one profile with a second owner
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first enumerate eligible binaries and unsupported dependencies; every eligible binary builds reproducibly for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl and runs in the declared clean cell; any no-/nix/store or static claim is proven from the artifact; every ineligible component has an explicit closure, bundle, AppImage-like, or documented host-Nix disposition; the one profile remains the install owner.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the source packaging and tests from this task, retain the pre-task profile generation, and invalidate portability claims; no installed profile or generated runtime is mutated as build proof.
columns.next_action: Begin after YZXCONV-003 and YZXCONV-013 establish the single-profile and four-repository source contracts.

columns.title: Make Home Manager a component of the one foundation install owner
columns.owner_agent: Yazelix Home Manager Agent / Runtime Verification Agent
columns.goal: Use TDD to package Home Manager inside the same lifeos_foundation_yzx profile element, materialize only reviewed Yazelix editable inputs, own the profile desktop entry and layout override, and prevent Home Manager, XDG profiles, generated runtime, or user-local launchers from becoming parallel owners.
columns.inputs: YZXCONV-002 ownership contract; YZXCONV-003 single-profile task; Yazelix home_manager source; active profile desktop contract; editable and generated path contract
columns.target_artifacts: Home Manager package contract; source and evaluation tests; desktop ownership test; migration and rollback receipt; installed single-owner proof
columns.blocked_paths: hand edits under /home/flexnetos/.local/share/yazelix/**; a second Nix profile element; stale /home/flexnetos/.local/bin/yzx; stale user-local desktop launchers; auth, sessions, databases, or secrets
columns.verification_gate: Red tests first fail when Home Manager is absent, selects a second profile, writes generated runtime as source, or allows a shadow launcher; Nix evaluation and build prove Home Manager is consumed by the one foundation element; the installed desktop entry launches profile yzx with the profile layout override; stale user-local shadows are absent; rollback restores the prior profile generation without moving agent state.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert the Home Manager source package and tests, reselect the checksum-recorded prior profile generation, and restore only reviewed editable inputs; never restore stale launchers or hand-edit generated runtime.
columns.next_action: Begin after the owner ratifies YZXCONV-002 and the single-profile contract in YZXCONV-003 is ready for source implementation.

columns.title: Enumerate every bash POSIX Zsh Fish and Xonsh command site as a reviewed inventory artifact
columns.goal: Produce the file-by-file inventory of every agent-stack shell command site (48 bash files: 20 Claude hooks sharing lib.sh, 23 lifecycle scripts, 2 skill scripts, 3 yazelix shell hooks) classified native-Nu vs route-through-rtk-proxy, plus a Fish and Xonsh discovery sweep, so YZXCONV-011 and YZXCONV-017 refactor from an enumerated list rather than a promised inventory.
columns.inputs: envctl home .claude and .codex hooks and settings; assets/scripts and scripts and home/bin; yazelix per-session shell hooks; agent-env-codex skill scripts
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/reports/shell_command_inventory.md; per-file native-vs-route classification; Fish and Xonsh sweep result
columns.blocked_paths: tracked product-source mutation; profile mutation; agent config mutation; generated Yazelix runtime mutation
columns.verification_gate: The inventory lists every active bash sh zsh fish xonsh command site with a path a native-Nu-vs-route-through class and the competing-wrapper flags; a repeat scan finds no unlisted shell command site under the inventoried trees.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the inventory report and its classification table from the convergence package; no product or config file is touched.
columns.next_action: Write the enumerated inventory report from the research findings and run the Fish and Xonsh discovery sweep.

columns.title: Pin the single Nushell wrapper chokepoint design decision
columns.goal: Record the binding decision that the profile-owned rtk_wrappers.nu is the single command-routing chokepoint (one executable owner no second command catalog no parallel wrapper binary or plugin bridge) and enumerate the four competing lanes to retire: shell_bash.sh shell_zsh.zsh the shell_nu.nu residue and the .claude/settings.local.json bash -lc permission strings.
columns.inputs: rtk_wrappers.nu; shell_bash.sh; shell_zsh.zsh; shell_nu.nu; .claude/settings.local.json
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/decisions.json one-wrapper entry; competing-lane retirement list
columns.blocked_paths: editing rtk_wrappers.nu or the competing lanes in this task; product-source mutation; profile mutation
columns.verification_gate: The decision names rtk_wrappers.nu as the sole chokepoint lists the four competing lanes with a retirement disposition each and is referenced by YZXCONV-010 verification; no second command catalog is introduced.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the one-wrapper decision entry and the retirement list; no wrapper source is changed.
columns.next_action: Author the decisions.json one-wrapper entry and cross-link it into YZXCONV-010 and YZXCONV-011.

columns.title: Enumerate the single-profile PATH binary checklist and install gaps
columns.goal: Produce the explicit required-binary checklist for the single nix profile (grit icm rtk git-kb meta weave flexnetos_runner envctl nu_plugin yzx rusty-idd teri hf) with resolve on-profile and has-nu-config status record the install gaps (envctl rusty-idd teri hf and the bare flexnetos_runner nu_plugin yazelix names) the PATH-ordering drift where the profile bin sits behind fifteen store bins and a second default profile and the disposition of the six asset workspace members as runtime-consumed not PATH binaries.
columns.inputs: nix-profile bin and toolbin listing; PATH entries; rtk_wrappers.nu nu-config coverage; six asset workspace member repos
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/reports/profile_binary_checklist.md; install-gap list; PATH-ordering-drift note; asset-member disposition
columns.blocked_paths: profile mutation; installing binaries; PATH mutation; product-source mutation
columns.verification_gate: The checklist enumerates every required binary with resolve on-profile and nu-config status lists each install gap and PATH-ordering-drift item and records an explicit disposition for each of the six asset workspace members; it feeds YZXCONV-003 YZXCONV-006 and YZXCONV-014 gates.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the checklist report; no profile or PATH state is changed.
columns.next_action: Write the profile-binary checklist from the research findings and confirm the asset-member disposition with the owner.

columns.title: Design strict Nushell command routing for both agent shell tools
columns.goal: Design the owner-ratified strict-nu-on-both-agents routing using the research-proven two-swaps-plus-hard-gate model: (1) SYNTAX SWAP a vendored nushell-syntax skill so each agent emits Nushell not bash; (2) INTERPRETER ROUTING a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer built-in nu --mcp from Nushell 0.108+, else a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate honoring the no-C boundary) into which the model writes nu as a typed argument; (3) HARD GATE deny the built-in Bash tool so no bash escape remains. The nu -c PreToolUse rewrite hook is REJECTED as primary: broken by claude-code #4535 (dev-null injection) and #15897/#26506 (updatedInput), and it hands bash syntax to a non-POSIX nu parser (verified locally on nu 0.113.1: andand, dollar-paren, 2>&1, heredocs all fail). Wrapper-lane convergence stays the documented partial fallback.
columns.inputs: nushell MCP options (nu --mcp, cablehead/mcp-server-nu, ck3mp3r/nu-mcp); nushell-pro and nushell-skills skill content; claude-code and codex shell issues; local nu 0.113.1 verification; owner decision 2026-07-14
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/decisions.json strict-nu-routing entry; per-agent MCP+skill+deny-Bash design; irreducible-floor note
columns.blocked_paths: editing agent hooks config or the profile in this task; agent config mutation; profile mutation
columns.verification_gate: The design specifies the nushell MCP execution path the vendored nu-syntax skill and the Bash-tool deny gate for both agents names the irreducible floor (feature-swap not shim: bash-emitting commands fail loudly, enforcement probabilistic on syntax and bounded on coverage) records the rejected nu -c rewrite with its failure mode cites the claude-code and codex issues and feeds YZXCONV-017 and YZXCONV-028; no unverified shell-override claim is made.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the routing design entry; no hook or agent config is changed.
columns.next_action: Verified 2026-07-14: profile Nushell 0.113.1 ships the mcp feature (nu --mcp, --mcp-transport stdio/http, --mcp-port present); finalize the MCP+skill+deny-Bash design for YZXCONV-028 on the built-in nu --mcp.

columns.title: Bring the agent runtime config files under the reproducible projection
columns.owner_agent: Codex Runtime Agent / Claude Runtime Agent
columns.goal: Close the reproducibility gap where the active ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.codex/config.toml are standalone runtime files that diverge from the envctl-home projected sources with a different model and a different hook set; specify that the projection and lock own or symlink these two runtime files so the rtk hook and future nu-routing cannot drift without moving auth or state out of ~/.claude and ~/.codex.
columns.inputs: active ~/.claude/settings.json; active ~/.codex/config.toml; envctl home .claude and .codex projected sources; agent-env lock
columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/reports/agent_config_projection_gap.md; ownership and symlink design for the two runtime files
columns.blocked_paths: editing ~/.claude or ~/.codex in this task; auth.json and credentials; sessions and databases; profile mutation
columns.verification_gate: The report proves the current divergence between the two standalone runtime files and their projected sources and specifies a projection or symlink ownership design that keeps auth and state in place; it feeds YZXCONV-004 and YZXCONV-005 materializer gates.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the gap report; no agent config auth or state is touched.
columns.next_action: Write the projection-gap report and fold the two runtime files into the YZXCONV-004 and YZXCONV-005 materializer design.

columns.title: Adopt the profile-owned Nushell MCP tool the nu-syntax skill and the Bash-tool deny gate
columns.goal: Implement the YZXCONV-026 design: package a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer building the profile nu with the mcp feature for nu --mcp else vendor a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate consistent with the no-C trust boundary), vendor a nushell-syntax skill into agent-skills so both Claude and Codex emit idiomatic nu, and add the deny of the built-in Bash tool to both agents through the envctl agent-env projection so nushell is the only sanctioned shell surface. TDD with negative tests that reject a bash escape and prove a nu command runs through the MCP tool.
columns.inputs: YZXCONV-026 design; profile nushell 0.113.1 mcp-feature status; cablehead/mcp-server-nu and ck3mp3r/nu-mcp references; nushell-pro and nushell-skills skill content; envctl agent-env source and lock
columns.target_artifacts: profile-owned Nushell MCP tool; vendored nu-syntax skill in agent-skills; Bash-tool deny agent-env projection; negative and positive routing tests
columns.blocked_paths: adding a C-linked dependency to the trust boundary; global package install; agent auth or state mutation; generated Yazelix runtime hand edits; applying agent-env without preview and review
columns.verification_gate: The profile exposes a Nushell MCP execution tool resolving through the one profile; both agents carry the vendored nu-syntax skill and a Bash-tool deny in their projected config; negative tests prove a bash command is rejected and a nu command runs through the MCP tool; the no-C boundary and single-profile ownership are preserved; a second agent-env preview is idempotent.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior agent-env source lock and projections through the envctl operation receipt re-enable the Bash tool and remove the MCP tool and skill; no auth or state is moved.
columns.next_action: Verified 2026-07-14: profile Nushell 0.113.1 ships the mcp feature (nu --mcp present) so no build or vendored crate is needed; implement on the built-in nu --mcp behind agent-env preview under owner approval.

columns.title: Reconcile agent-env meta and yazelix integration drift and ownership gaps
columns.goal: Fix the config-level drift the 2026-07-14 integration audit surfaced: (a) fix the .meta.yaml stale claude-plugins/codex-plugins peers and the meta-plugins path drift (executed by YZXCONV-030);(b) reconcile the Codex MCP drift where the active ~/.codex/config.toml carries an unaccounted hand-added openaiDeveloperDocs and is missing the agent-env exa, diverging from both the agent-env-generated envctl/.codex/config.toml and the projected home/.codex/config.toml; (c) update the stale CLAUDE.md MCP baseline that still names the retired github context7 memory playwright sequential-thinking servers (enforced baseline is exa cognitum-seed cognitum); (d) wire the unwired standalone meta_mcp server (executed by YZXCONV-031);(e) keep the loop_lib $SHELL -c Plan path POSIX-safe or nu-overlay-free under the nushell-only mission (feeds YZXCONV-017).
columns.inputs: integration audit 2026-07-14 findings; .meta.yaml; envctl agent-env.yaml and lock; active and projected codex config; CLAUDE.md
columns.target_artifacts: corrected .meta.yaml; reconciled Codex MCP baseline; refreshed CLAUDE.md; meta_mcp wiring decision; loop_lib shell-safety note
columns.blocked_paths: deleting the archived plugin tarballs; agent auth or state mutation; profile mutation; applying agent-env without preview and review
columns.verification_gate: After the fix: .meta.yaml lists no stale claude-plugins or codex-plugins peers and the meta-plugins path resolves; envctl agent lock --check passes with the Codex baseline matching agent-env (exa cognitum-seed cognitum) and no unaccounted openaiDeveloperDocs; the CLAUDE.md MCP baseline matches agent-env.yaml; a meta_mcp wire-vs-leave decision is recorded; the loop_lib $SHELL -c path is documented POSIX-safe under nushell-only.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior .meta.yaml and config projections from the pre-change copies; re-enable any removed peer only from its published receipt; no auth or state is moved.
columns.next_action: Drop the stale plugin peers and fix the meta-plugins path in .meta.yaml, reconcile the Codex MCP baseline through agent-env, and refresh the CLAUDE.md baseline under owner review.

columns.title: Fix the .meta.yaml stale plugin peers and the meta-plugins path drift
columns.goal: Drop the stale claude-plugins and codex-plugins peer entries from /home/flexnetos/meta/.meta.yaml - their content is archived-only under meta/_archive and the codex payloads migrated into meta-plugins, so leaving them lets a meta git update re-clone the retired repos and resurrect the duplication - and correct the meta-plugins entry whose declared path src/meta-plugins does not exist while the live checkout is at the meta root (either drop the path to match the other root-checked-out non-code peers and the .gitignore root-form ignore, or move the checkout into src/). Preserve the archived tarballs.
columns.inputs: integration audit 2026-07-14; /home/flexnetos/meta/.meta.yaml; meta/_archive plugin tarballs; meta/.gitignore; meta_core config parser (walk_meta_tree, check_orphan_status)
columns.target_artifacts: corrected .meta.yaml with no stale peers and a resolvable meta-plugins path; before and after meta project list receipt
columns.blocked_paths: deleting the archived plugin tarballs; deleting the live meta-plugins checkout; cloning or removing peer repos without owner approval
columns.verification_gate: meta project list (or meta_core walk_meta_tree) shows no claude-plugins or codex-plugins peers and a resolvable meta-plugins path; a meta git update dry-run would not re-clone the retired repos; the archived tarballs are untouched.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior .meta.yaml from the pre-change copy; re-add any removed peer only from its published receipt.
columns.next_action: Remove the claude-plugins and codex-plugins peer blocks and fix the meta-plugins path in .meta.yaml under owner review, then confirm with meta project list.

columns.title: Wire the meta_mcp server into the agent MCP baseline via a profile-owned frontdoor
columns.goal: Make the standalone meta_mcp server (bin meta-mcp, ~30 fleet tools over stdio JSON-RPC, meta_cli-backed) an active agent MCP. Because meta-mcp is a LOCAL stdio binary and agent-env only generates URL-only MCPs that launch no non-profile local binary (the yazelix-mirror rule), wiring it requires FIRST giving it a profile-owned frontdoor: package meta-mcp into the single nix profile (like rtk and yzx), then add it to agent-env.yaml so envctl agent sync projects it identically into .mcp.json (Claude) and .codex/config.toml (Codex), passing the yazelix-mirror gate with a profile-owned command rather than a bunx or other non-profile launcher. TDD.
columns.inputs: meta_mcp crate (bin meta-mcp, ~30 tools); agent-env.yaml yazelix-mirror rule + scripts/tests/test-agent-mcp-yazelix-mirror.sh; yazelix flake packaging; envctl agent-env engine
columns.target_artifacts: profile-packaged meta-mcp frontdoor; agent-env.yaml meta-mcp entry; projected .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml meta-mcp server; mirror-gate proof
columns.blocked_paths: adding a non-profile local-binary MCP that fails the yazelix-mirror gate; global install; agent auth or state mutation; applying agent-env without preview and review
columns.verification_gate: meta-mcp resolves through the single nix profile; agent-env.yaml lists meta-mcp and envctl agent sync projects it identically into .mcp.json and .codex/config.toml; the yazelix-mirror test passes (profile-owned frontdoor, no non-profile launcher); the meta-mcp initialize and tools-list handshake succeeds from the agent; a second sync preview is idempotent.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the meta-mcp agent-env entry and profile package via the envctl operation receipt; restore the prior lock and projections; meta-mcp stays a standalone unwired binary.
columns.next_action: Package meta-mcp into the single profile, then add it to agent-env.yaml and prove the yazelix-mirror gate plus identical Claude and Codex projection under owner approval.

columns.title: Bring the yazelix agent.command and agent.args launch keys under agent-env ownership
columns.owner_agent: Codex Runtime Agent / Claude Runtime Agent
columns.goal: Connect agent-env to the yazelix managed-agent launch surface: the profile-owned yazelix popup launches the agent via the contract-checked agent.command (executable-or-auto) and agent.args (string array) keys (yzx-config catalog.rs:8-9, root_config.rs, runtime/yzx/runtime.rs:34-35). Make agent-env the single declarative owner of these values (which agent binary launches, with which args) so the launch is reproducible and not hand-edited - materialized from a reviewed agent-env input into the yazelix config surface (programs.yazelix.config.settings.agent.command/agent.args via Home Manager, or the ~/.config/yazelix input), consistent with the YZXCONV-004/005 config materialization. TDD.
columns.inputs: yazelix agent.command/agent.args schema (yzx-config catalog.rs, root_config.rs, runtime/yzx/runtime.rs, ARCHITECTURE.md:113-114); agent-env engine + agent-env.yaml; yazelix HM module config.settings; YZXCONV-004/005 materializer design
columns.target_artifacts: agent-env-owned agent.command/agent.args input; materializer into the yazelix config; reproducibility test
columns.blocked_paths: hand-editing generated yazelix runtime; agent auth or state mutation; profile mutation; applying without preview and review
columns.verification_gate: agent.command and agent.args are materialized from a single reviewed agent-env-owned input (not hand-edited); yzx doctor/status reports the intended agent.command/agent.args; a second sync is idempotent; the value checksums back to the agent-env input.
columns.rollback_plan: Restore the prior yazelix agent config from the checksum-backed copy; no auth or state moved.
columns.next_action: Design the agent-env-owned agent.command/agent.args input and its materializer into the yazelix config, folded into the YZXCONV-004/005 runtime-config work.

columns.title: Package the remaining agent supply-chain binaries as flake-declared yazelix runtime packages
columns.goal: Extend the single-profile runtime the yazelix way - each binary is a flake output AND a declared runtime package (as rtk is), not merely present on PATH. Package the remaining agent supply chain into the one profile: claude, codex, grit, icm, weave, bun, bunx, plus the install-gap binaries from YZXCONV-025 (envctl, rusty-idd, teri, hf, nu_plugin, flexnetos_runner) and any remaining supply-chain tools. Verify each resolves through ~/.nix-profile as a flake-declared runtime package (a flake packages.<system>.<name> output consumed by the runtime closure), closing the PATH-ordering drift so the profile front door - not an earlier standalone store bin - owns them. TDD packaging tests.
columns.inputs: YZXCONV-025 profile-binary checklist and install gaps; yazelix flake.nix packaging (rtk and yzx as the pattern); the runtime closure (mkYzx); the PATH-ordering-drift finding
columns.target_artifacts: flake package outputs and runtime-closure entries for each binary; per-binary profile-resolution proof; PATH-ordering-drift closure
columns.blocked_paths: global package-manager install; user-local bin shadows; profile mutation before build proof; generated Yazelix runtime hand edits
columns.verification_gate: Each listed binary (claude, codex, grit, icm, weave, bun, bunx, plus the YZXCONV-025 install gaps) is a flake packages output and a declared runtime-closure package; command -v resolves each through ~/.nix-profile/bin or toolbin (not an earlier standalone store bin); red-then-green packaging tests prove reproducible builds; the one profile remains the sole install owner.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the flake packaging additions and tests; reselect the prior profile generation; no user-local shadow is introduced.
columns.next_action: Enumerate the exact binary set from YZXCONV-025, then add flake package outputs and runtime-closure entries the yazelix way (rtk as the template) and prove profile resolution under owner approval.

columns.title: Build the three-lane supply-chain catalog (no-optional, latest-npm ruvector)
columns.goal: Create supply_chain.catalog.csv beside task_graph.source.csv as a SEPARATE registry with a one-way task_id foreign key INTO the task graph (the task graph is never edited and never given supply-chain columns; the reverse task-to-package index is built at generation time). Model three lanes, all yazelix-nix-profile-owned or project-local-pinned and never system or global: path (agent CLIs and tools resolved on PATH via the yazelix runtime closure), service (PostgreSQL plus the RuVector extension, which MUST be flake-declared IN the yazelix nix-profile and run as a profile-owned daemon), and app-dep (libraries the app links, pinned in the app lockfile via a profile-owned toolchain). Keep two independent axes: source_registry (where latest lives) versus install_method (how it is built). Enforce the NO-OPTIONAL LAW: there is no optional, later, or non-blocking state; every row is built before DONE and an agent that defers a row has entered a failure loop; the token optional is illegal as a classification value. Seed ruvector as npm-latest (bun add) in the app-dep lane and as the profile-owned PostgreSQL extension in the service lane; local meta-ruvector and crates.io are reference and lagging fallbacks only. Registry keys come from the secretd broker at build time, never the nix store.
columns.inputs: yazelix_runtime_convergence/SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md; DEC-YZXCONV-007; Architecture Blueprint - LifeOS Core Foundation.md (sha256 014bbebb) lines 87-113; COGNITUM guides (open reference); meta-ruvector plus worktrees (reference); YZXCONV-025 install-gap checklist; YZXCONV-033 packaging pattern
columns.target_artifacts: supply_chain.catalog.csv source of truth; generated/supply_chain.catalog.json; the gen-time task-to-package reverse index; per-lane install-method mapping
columns.blocked_paths: system or global package-manager installs; forcing an app-dep or service item into the path lane or vice versa; nix-profile mutation before build proof; deleting the COGNITUM reference media; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: The catalog validates: every row has a lane in path/service/app-dep, a source_registry, and an install_method; the token optional never appears as a classification value; every task_id foreign key resolves to an existing task; the ruvector rows are source_registry npm with install_method bun-add-latest (app-dep) and a profile-owned PostgreSQL extension (service); no row is in a deferred or optional state; generated/supply_chain.catalog.json regenerates deterministically and the reverse task-to-package index builds.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove supply_chain.catalog.csv and its generated JSON and SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md; the task graph is untouched by construction (one-way foreign key), so no task-CSV rollback is needed.
columns.next_action: Scaffold supply_chain.catalog.csv with the three-lane schema and the rtk and ruvector seed rows, wire the gen-time reverse join, then enumerate further from the open COGNITUM and meta-ruvector references as needed.

columns.title: Package PostgreSQL and the RuVector extension INTO the yazelix nix-profile (service lane)
columns.owner_agent: Yazelix Packaging Agent / Database Runtime Agent
columns.goal: Close the owner-identified current-state gap that no database is in the yazelix nix-profile: the databases MUST be in the yazelix nix-profile to pull this off. Declare PostgreSQL and the RuVector PostgreSQL extension as flake outputs in the yazelix runtime closure (the same the-yazelix-way mechanism as rtk), so the single profile owns the whole stack (blueprint Phase 4 build the entire stack, musl-static, drop the nix store). RuVector loads as a profile-provided extension into the profile-provided postgres via shared_preload_libraries with a data dir kept outside the store. Latest RuVector is the npm-published build (200-plus agent tools); crates.io lags and local meta-ruvector is the napi-exhaust scratchpad reference only. No system postgres, no apt, no global install. TDD packaging tests.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint lines 68-79 (RuVector as a PostgreSQL extension) and 23-42 (Phase 4 Nix plus musl); yazelix flake.nix packaging (rtk and yzx as the pattern) and the runtime closure (mkYzx); SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md service lane; DEC-YZXCONV-007; the latest RuVector npm and extension build
columns.target_artifacts: flake packages outputs for postgresql and the ruvector extension; yazelix runtime-closure entries; a profile-owned postgres-plus-ruvector service proof; a data-dir-outside-the-store layout
columns.blocked_paths: system or apt postgres; global installs; profile mutation before build proof; committing a database data dir or secrets into the repo or the nix store; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: postgresql and the ruvector extension are flake packages outputs and declared yazelix runtime-closure packages; both resolve through the nix profile (not a system or apt postgres); a profile-owned postgres starts with the ruvector extension loaded (present in pg_available_extensions and CREATE EXTENSION ruvector succeeds) against a data dir outside the store; red-then-green packaging tests prove a reproducible build; the one profile is the sole install owner.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert only the flake packaging additions and tests and reselect the prior profile generation; leave any existing external database untouched; no system postgres is introduced.
columns.next_action: After the catalog service-lane rows exist, add flake package outputs for postgresql and the ruvector extension and a runtime-closure entry the yazelix way, then prove CREATE EXTENSION ruvector from a profile-owned postgres under owner approval.

columns.title: Seed and freeze the full store inventory as the catalog-before-run target gate (per-artifact verified leading registry)
columns.goal: Populate AND FREEZE supply_chain.catalog.csv with the complete verified store inventory (SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8) as the CATALOG-BEFORE-RUN target gate: per owner ratification DEC-YZXCONV-009 the supply-chain target for every artifact is set in the catalog BEFORE any downstream supply-chain task (035, 037, 038) executes, because the source with the latest code wins and the code is the source of truth. Add every required row across three lanes. service: SC-postgres (the consolidation target and permanent source of truth), SC-ruvector-postgres (the pgrx extension carrying the 200-plus SQL agent tools, loaded into postgres), SC-libsql-remote (secrets store, remote-only no-C), and SC-ruvllm pending its lane ruling. app-dep: SC-redb with the DUAL-MODE note (pass-through in the happy path and a durable retaining WAL when postgres is down, plus dedup and geometry-engine roles), SC-ruvector-core, SC-ruvector-sona, SC-ruvector-npm as the ONLY npm-latest-governed binding, SC-rvf for AgentDB and the rvf format, SC-hnsw-rs, SC-sqlx-sqlite, SC-candle-or-ort, and SC-fastembed. path: SC-nu-plugin-ruvector, SC-beads, SC-weave (dual sqlite and libsql), and SC-embed-minilm as a model asset. For EVERY row record the VERIFIED LEADING REGISTRY resolved live at pin time (crates.io leads the ruvector engine; npm carries only the front-end binding; the at-ruvnet-slash-ruvector handle is dead) into source_registry plus source_locator plus pinned_version, keep classification required for every row, and add audit rows for kache (compiler cache, not a data store) and the codex-harness rusqlite-bundled C-SQLite caveat so the no-C boundary stays auditable. Downstream tasks CONSUME this frozen target and never re-derive it; a task that installs, packages, or links an artifact whose catalog target is unset or unverified is a NO-OPTIONAL black-hole violation and fails closed.
columns.inputs: SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8 inventory; DEC-YZXCONV-007 and DEC-YZXCONV-008 and DEC-YZXCONV-009 (owner catalog-before-run ratification); the 2026-07-14 deep-research reports (codebase store scan, blueprint cross-ref, live registry verification); Architecture Blueprint lines 44-48 and 245-252 and 361-439
columns.target_artifacts: populated and frozen supply_chain.catalog.csv covering all lanes with a per-artifact pinned_version; a catalog freeze marker (pinned_at plus per-row verified registry); generated/supply_chain.catalog.json; the task-to-package reverse index
columns.blocked_paths: promoting the codex-harness bundled C-SQLite into the profile; forcing an item into the wrong lane; applying the npm-latest rule to independent crates such as redb candle sqlx or libsql; system or global installs; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: supply_chain.catalog.csv contains every SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN section 8 row with a valid lane and source_registry and install_method; EVERY row carries a source_locator and a pinned_version verified live at pin time (per-artifact leading registry, never a blanket npm rule); the catalog is frozen before any downstream supply-chain task runs so 035 and 037 and 038 consume the pinned target and never re-derive it; redb carries the dual-mode WAL note; ruvector-postgres is the service-lane 200-plus-SQL-tool extension; SC-ruvector-npm is the only row on the npm-latest rule and uses a live handle and never at-ruvnet-slash-ruvector; no row is optional or deferred; the kache and rusqlite-bundled audit rows are present and flagged; generated/supply_chain.catalog.json regenerates deterministically and the reverse index builds.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the added catalog rows and regenerate; the task graph is untouched by construction (one-way foreign key).
columns.next_action: Write the section-8 rows into supply_chain.catalog.csv with per-artifact verified registries and pinned versions and the dual-mode redb note, freeze the target, then regenerate the catalog JSON and reverse index so 035 037 and 038 consume the frozen pins.

columns.title: Rule the ruvllm lane and package the edge-inference runtime into the profile if it is a service
columns.owner_agent: Database Runtime Agent / Yazelix Packaging Agent
columns.goal: Resolve the open lane ruling for ruvllm, the edge inference engine that keeps a single frozen foundation model in shared memory for many agents (blueprint lines 274-282). Decide whether ruvllm is a linked app-dep crate or a profile-owned serving daemon in the path or service lane; the blueprint daemon shape argues for a profile-owned runtime like PostgreSQL got in YZXCONV-035. If it is a service or path binary, add a flake output and a yazelix runtime-closure entry and prove the frozen model loads in shared memory; if it is purely a linked library, record it as an app-dep crate pin. Apply the per-artifact verified-registry law (ruvllm 2.3.0 currently leads on crates.io). TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint lines 13 and 274-282 and 314; SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8a and section 9 ruling 1; DEC-YZXCONV-008; yazelix flake.nix packaging pattern (rtk and postgres as templates); ruvllm 2.3.0 crates.io
columns.target_artifacts: a recorded ruvllm lane decision; if service or path a flake output and runtime-closure entry plus a shared-memory model-load proof; the corresponding catalog row updated
columns.blocked_paths: global installs; profile mutation before build proof; leaving the lane ambiguous; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: the ruvllm lane is recorded with a rationale; if service or path ruvllm is a flake packages output and a declared runtime-closure package resolving through the nix profile and a frozen model loads in shared memory in a red-then-green test; if app-dep the crate pin is recorded with the verified registry; the catalog row matches the decision.
columns.rollback_plan: Revert the flake packaging additions and tests and reselect the prior profile generation; leave the recorded decision as a documented note.
columns.next_action: After the catalog inventory exists, rule the ruvllm lane against the blueprint daemon shape and if service or path package it into the profile the yazelix way under owner approval.

columns.title: Provision the local embedding model asset and inference framework as a reproducible runtime artifact
columns.goal: Provision the local embedding stack the envctl bridge needs so the ingestion embedding step is provisioned rather than assumed (blueprint line 161 states the database does not generate embeddings; a separate local model does). Package the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 model weights as a reproducible content-addressed runtime asset and pin the inference framework (Candle or ORT per blueprint line 575) as a normal crates.io app-dep, not under the ruvector npm-latest rule. The asset is fetched reproducibly and kept outside the nix store data path; no global install. It is install-now, never deferrable (NO-OPTIONAL law). TDD.
columns.inputs: Architecture Blueprint lines 161 and 386 and 409 and 575; SUPPLY-CHAIN-DESIGN.md section 8b rows SC-candle and SC-embed-minilm; DEC-YZXCONV-008; the envctl bridge embedding path
columns.target_artifacts: a reproducible all-MiniLM-L6-v2 asset fetch with a pinned digest; the Candle or ORT framework crate pin; catalog rows SC-embed-minilm and SC-candle finalized
columns.blocked_paths: committing model weights into the repo or the nix store; global installs; applying the npm-latest rule to the framework crate; agent auth or state mutation
columns.verification_gate: the all-MiniLM-L6-v2 asset is fetched reproducibly against a pinned digest and resolves outside the store data path; the Candle or ORT framework is pinned from crates.io; the SC-embed-minilm and SC-candle catalog rows are finalized as required; a red-then-green test proves the embedding step can load the model.
columns.rollback_plan: Remove the asset-fetch and framework pin and the two catalog rows; no store data path is mutated.
columns.next_action: After the inventory rows exist, pin the Candle or ORT framework and add a reproducible digest-pinned all-MiniLM-L6-v2 asset fetch under owner approval.
